attribution.text: Route, junction, and history details adapted from the Wikipedia article "Trail of the Ancients", by Wikipedia contributors, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

paragraphs: The Trail of the Ancients was made a Utah state byway about 1990. The Trail of the Ancients Scenic and Historic Byway was designated a Colorado Scenic and Historic Byway in 1994. The Trail of the Ancients was designated a National Scenic Byway on September 22, 2005 by the U.S. Secretary of Transportation and the Federal Highway Administration. The designation was the first National Scenic Byway that totally focused its sites' archaeological qualities.[b]

references.text: At the intersection of State Road-41, the northwesterly road leads to the Utah section of the Trail of the Ancients

Senate Bill 1414 was introduced on July 15, 2005 calling for a "study of the suitability and feasibility of establishing the Trail of the Ancients National Heritage Area in the Four Corners region of the States of Utah, Colorado, Arizona, and New Mexico." The Heritage Area was to

gallery.cap: Balcony House at Mesa Verde National Park
gallery.desc: A tour group stops to learn about and admire the Balcony House at Mesa Verde National Park. Location: Mesa Verde National Park, Colorado (37.165° N 108.474° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Sally Pearce.
Tucked away under a sandstone overhang, you can see the remains of a pueblo community. Location: Trail of the Ancients, Utah (37.184° N 108.487° W) Status: Public domain.
For hundreds of years this niche has protected these American Indian cliff dwellings. Location: Butler Wash Indian Ruins, Utah (37.184° N 108.487° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Susan Taylor

gallery.cap: Cliff Palace at Mesa Verde National Park
gallery.desc: Cliff Palace, the most well-known archaeological site in Mesa Verde National Park, is the largest cliff dwelling in North America. Location: Colorado (37.165° N 108.474° W) Status: Public domain. Photo supplied by Mesa Verde Country

gallery.cap: Cliff Palace at Mesa Verde National Park
gallery.desc: The largest cliff dwelling in North America, Cliff Palace is one of the major ruins in Mesa Verde National Park. The buildings were constructed primarily using sandstone, mortar, and wooden beams. Location: Mesa Verde National Park, Colorado (37.184° N 108.487° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of National Park Service - Mesa Verde National Park

Almost imperceptible from this distance, in the top pocket of the rock are ancient cliff dwellings. The shot lends perspective to the discreet placement of the settlement. Location: Trail of the Ancients, Mesa Verde National Park, Utah (37.183° N 108.487° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: D shaped tower at Hovenweep National Monument
gallery.desc: Around 1200 AD, thousands of stones were piled to form this "D" shaped tower, a form not seen often in the Four Corners region. The builders of the towers at Hovenweep used this shape, along with square and round towers. Location: Hovenweep National Monument, Trail of the Ancients in Utah, Utah (37.386° N 109.081° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of National Park Service, Hovenweep National Monu

gallery.cap: Dancer at the Utah Navajo Fair
gallery.desc: This young woman is preparing for her turn on the dance floor, a pounded dirt area adjacent to a covered drumming area, at the Utah Navajo Fair, held each fall in Bluff. One of the most exciting events at the fair is the fancy dress dance competition. Location: Bluff, Utah (37.284° N 109.554° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of Utah's Canyon Country Visitor Services

Rivulets of golden sand, formed by the wind, crawl towards the horizon. In the background, totems rise off of the desert floor. Location: Along Trail of the Ancients, Utah (37.283° N 109.594° W) Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of Utah's Canyon Country Visitor Services

gallery.cap: Feather Sash at Edge of the Cedars State Park Museum
gallery.desc: Found on the Abajo Mountains near Blanding, this sash is made of macaw feathers which were imported by the Puebloan Indians from Mexico. The sash is in excellent condition and is on view at the museum at the Edge of the Cedars State Park and Museum. Location: Cedars State Park and Museum, Utah (37.631° N 109.490° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of Edge of the Cedars State Park Museum

gallery.cap: Flags on the Four Corners Monument
gallery.desc: A slight breeze lifts the colorful flags that surround the Four Corners Monument, the only place where four states, Utah, New Mexico, Arizona, and Colorado, meet. Location: Four Corners Monument, Utah (36.999° N 109.045° W) Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of Utah's Canyon Country Visitor Services

A raft lazily floats by beautiful, sandstone cliffs. Float trips travel the San Juan River from Bluff to Mexican Hat and beyond to Lake Powell. Location: San Juan River, Trail of the Ancients, Utah (37.218° N 110.025° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of Wild Rivers Expeditions (Tim Tyson)
Tucked under an overhanging rock, look for the window of the one-story home. Location: Trail of the Ancients, Mesa Verde National Park, Utah (37.184° N 108.487° W) Status: Public domain.

Horseback tours travel through Monument Valley to spots a vehicle cannot access. "The Mittens," a landmark along the trail, looms in the background in this historic photo. Location: Monument Valley, Utah Status: Public domain. Courtesy of David Boyer

gallery.cap: Horsecollar Ruin at Natural Bridges National Monument
gallery.desc: The photo shows the granaries as well as the distinctive doorways of Horsecollar Ruin, named because the doorways look like collars used on horse harnesses. Notice the large stones used as a "foundation" under the smaller stones. Location: Natural Bridges National Monument, Trail of the Ancients, Utah (37.598° N 110.029° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of National Park Service, Natural Bridges

Located in both Colorado and Utah, this National Monument is characterized by square, oval, circular, and D-shaped towers built by Puebloans around 1200 AD. Location: Hovenweep National Monument, Trail of the Ancients, Colorado (37.410° N 109.030° W) Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Hovenweep National Monument Square Tower Ruin
gallery.desc: Taken from the canyon below, in the distance is the Square Tower Ruin. Location: Hovenweep National Monument, Utah (37.386° N 109.081° W) Status: Public domain. Courtesy of National Park Service, Hovenweep National Monument

gallery.cap: Indian Dancers at the Cortez Cultural Center
gallery.desc: Indian Dances and Cultural Programs are performed several nights a week at the Cortez Cultural Center. They are free to the public and demonstrate the rich Native American culture of the area. Location: Cortez, Colorado (37.476° N 108.546° W) Status: Public domain. Photo by Branson Reynolds
